title: Hydraulic actuator enabling to control dorsiflexion and plantar flexion speeds of the prosthetic foot joint
abstract: The invention relates to a hydraulic actuator enabling to control the dorsiflexion and plantar flexion speeds that are movements of the prosthetic ankle joint.In its most basic form, said hydraulic actuator has a hole formed on the cylinder body (14) in the compression of the piston operated in at least two flow holes formed in the actuator body, at least one short check valve (5) that provides flow out of the piston and long check valve (1) that is operated by passing through another flow hole formed on the cylinder body (14) when the piston is extended.
claims: Claims 1.A hydraulic actuator enabling to control dorsiflexion and plantar flexion speeds that are movements of the prosthetic artificial ankle joint, formed on an artificial foot, that contains at least one piston mechanism within its structure to mimic ankle movements, characterized by comprising; â¢ at least a short check valve (5) that provides flow out of the piston by means of the hole formed on the cylinder body (14) when the piston operated inside at least two holes formed within the actuator body, is carrying out a compression process,and a long check valve (1) that is operated by passing over another flow hole that has been formed on the cylinder body (14) when the piston extends. â¢ at least 2 adjustment valves (7) that can control the piston extension and compression speeds separately, â¢ at least one accumulator piston (9) for providing excess volume formed within the system. 2. A hydraulic actuator according to claim 1, characterized by comprising; â¢ a long check valve (1) positioned in a flow hole formed on a lateral wall of the hydraulic actuator, â¢ a short check valve (5) positioned inside the flow hole formed on the other lateral wall opposite said wall, â¢ adjustment valves (7) positioned on one of the lateral walls adjacent to the lateral wall on which the check valves (1,5) are positioned, and â¢ an accumulator piston (9) positioned on one of the upper and lower walls adjacent to the lateral wall on which the check valves (1, 5) are positioned. 3. A hydraulic actuator according to claim 1, characterized in that it comprises a short check valve (5) equipped with an o-ring (2), a stopper (3) and a pressure spring (4). 4. A hydraulic actuator according to claim 1, characterized in that it comprises a long check valve (1) equipped with an o-ring (2), a stopper (3) and a pressure spring (4). 5. A hydraulic actuator according to claim 1, characterized in that it comprises an accumulator piston (9) equipped with a hydraulic seal (8), an accumulator pressure spring (10), a cover (11), an NBR stopper (12) and a setscrew (13). 6. A hydraulic actuator according to claim 1, characterized in that it comprises an adjustment element o-ring (6) equipped with an adjustment element o-ring (6). 7. A hydraulic actuator according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that it comprises as a single piece, a long check valve (1) and/or a short check valve (5) and an adjustment valve (7) that controls the flow formed in said valves.
